title: Simple Kayak/SUP Storage Rack
summary: Plans to build an extremely simple but useful kayak and/or SUP (stand-up paddleboard) storage rack.
description: Plans to build an extremely simple but useful kayak and/or SUP (stand-up paddleboard) storage rack. No fancy cuts, this piece is simply a rectangle frame made with 2 x 4’s and 4 x 4’s, wood glue, and pocket screws. Add hooks to organize your paddling accessories. This is a great beginner project.

steps.title: Cut
steps.body: Cut wood to sizes specified using miter saw, circular saw, or table saw.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Using a Kreg Jig drill pocket holes into ends of each cut 2" x 4" piece

steps.title: Assembly
steps.body: This is a very simple and basic build. Simply assemble the rack using wood glue and 2-1/2" pocket screws. You are building a frame of a rectangle box.

steps.title: Load it up!
steps.body: Load up the rack with kayaks on the bottom and paddleboards on the top. Add hooks to the rack for hanging PFDs, drybags, etc.
